tracker_meta.title: Housing Committee Progress Tracker
tracker_meta.subtitle: Meeting-by-meeting record of Housing Committee work toward the July 2026 Housing Action Plan recommendation to City Council
tracker_meta.audience: Housing Committee members and Planning Department staff
tracker_meta.source_note: This tracker is a working synthesis. Authoritative records are the official meeting minutes filed with the City Clerk. Markdown summaries in the /meeting-notes/ folder are working summaries prepared by Progress Portsmouth from the meeting recordings and draft minutes — they condense discussion and capture decisions but are not the official record. The Housing Committee Ranking List (working draft, May 2026) is a Planning Department document combining the Apr 23 staff shortlist with the May 21 straw poll and later signals; it is itself a draft, not yet adopted. Verify against the official minutes before relying on any decision recorded here.

meetings.title: Apr 23, 2026 — First meeting; HAP framing & staff shortlist presented

decisions.label: Tabor reviewed prior-year accomplishments: Sherburne School RFP (PHA selected), City property walks for housing parcels, Housing Trust Fund creation ($500K balance), first GNOD off Commerce Way, Land Use Board Commercial-to-Mixed-Use rezoning

Tabor identified five key issues the HAP should address: (1) Permitting to relieve land use board friction, (2) Incentives and financial tools to fund the Housing Trust, (3) City-owned land opportunities (e.g., City Hall lower lot), (4) Partnerships and capacity, (5) Education and outreach with data collection on best practices (Housing NH)

Committee discussed at length what opportunities the new 79-E might offer. Notes that 79-E does not apply to older properties that might be kept as workforce housing with an incentive to do so. Committee suggested a work session to create an income-restricted structure to present to Council. Next step: discuss with Legal and Tax Assessor.
Britz presented Planning Department staff rationale for selecting 26 feasible action items from the 147-item Progress Portsmouth list, classified short-term / long-term with staff comments

Committee agreed each member would bring their 'top 3' and 'top 10' from the staff shortlist — plus any items from the original 147-item plan that should also be prioritized — for discussion at the May 21 meeting

Public Comment — Gerry Duffy (Progress Portsmouth): (1) PP has a 'tough to build' explainer at progressportsmouth.com/tough-to-build; (2) recommended getting more input from those most affected by the workforce housing shortage, who tend not to attend public meetings

meetings.title: May 21, 2026 — Member straw poll & 79-E bucket framing

decisions.label: Member straw poll on top three priorities — each member identified personal priorities from the full 147-item Progress Portsmouth list
Stith introduced '79-E bucket' (anchor I-4) and 'Zoning Audit bucket' (anchor Z-21) as structural framing for organizing related items
Moreau clarified Portsmouth lacks municipal authority for tax relief outside RSA 674 categories (solar, elderly, veterans, disabled); RSA 79-E identified as a viable creative interpretation
Anderson flagged 10 additional items from the full Progress Portsmouth list as warranting inclusion (stated 12, listed 10 — two not identified)
Recurring meeting time confirmed: second Thursday of the month, 5:30 pm. Stith confirmed Zoning Audit (Z-21) now underway, will feed both the Housing Action Plan and the Master Plan.

Samonas raised three meta points: (1) completed items shouldn't be listed as priorities, (2) Plan should focus on best use of available land (only 2% available), (3) Plan should address audiences with no current inventory match (older couples downsizing, etc.)

meetings.title: June 11, 2026 — Scheduled
meetings.summary: Next regular Housing Committee meeting. Recurring second-Thursday slot at 5:30 pm.

interim_activity.label: Housing Committee Ranking List released
interim_activity.details: Working draft (Planning Department, May 2026) synthesizing May 21 straw poll with later signals. Replaces the May 21 raw straw poll as the canonical committee_priority record. Schema change: committee_priority now {votes, rank, source}. 11 items scored with explicit ranks. Z-21 retitled 'Comprehensive zoning audit' → 'Comprehensive zoning ordinance update.' Z-18 (room rental) and Z-19 (seasonal worker housing) marked complete — already addressed by existing ordinances. New staff_term assignments on Z-13, Z-17, SP-2.
